groups.theme: People evaluating Medito mainly for free access, optional donations, and the absence of ads or subscriptions
example_reviews: Truly Free and Incredibly High Quality: This app is an absolute gem. In a world where every free app is filled with ads or expensive subscriptions, Medito is actually free.
used this app years ago and was impressed with the no ads no subscription experience.
groups.notes: Trimmed the oversized automated cluster to reviews that clearly focus on free access, optional donations, and the lack of ads or subscriptions.

groups.theme: People using Medito at bedtime for sleep stories, sleep meditations, and uninterrupted overnight playback
example_reviews: I've been using Medito for a few months, and it really helps me sleep better. The sleep meditations, calming sounds, and Sleep Stories are amazing.
I use this free app for the sleep stories, to help me get back to sleep when I wake in the night.
groups.notes: Kept reviews that specifically mention bedtime use, sleep stories, sleep meditations, or requests for longer or smoother sleep playback, and removed broader relaxation comments.

groups.theme: People relying on Medito for anxiety, stress, panic, grief, and other emotionally difficult moments
example_reviews: I'm really really thankful I stumbled upon medito app. As a guy who has severe anxiety and stress, the guided meditation tremendously helped me.
Medito is truly a gem for anyone looking to build a calm and mindful routine. The app offers sections like Anger, Panic, Fear, Grief, Stress, and Sleep.
groups.notes: Narrowed the automated cluster to reviews that clearly describe emotional support needs rather than broad praise for mental wellness in general.

groups.theme: Beginners and returning users building a meditation habit through courses, streaks, reminders, and visible progress
example_reviews: The medito app is my top recommendation for anyone starting with meditation. There are courses that help demystify meditation for beginners.
It's the great app to meditate here. But here's the problem: The streak freezers.
groups.notes: Reduced the automated cluster to beginner-course, streak, challenge, timer, and habit-building reviews, and removed broader comments that were mostly about free access.

groups.theme: Regular users affected by crashes, broken playback, failed downloads or search, and progress problems after updates
example_reviews: I just want to complain about the search feature. From past 15 to 20 days the search button is not working.
Since the last update, the app no longer works. It runs a meditation audio once, but then stops working.
groups.notes: Kept the update-related reliability complaints and removed mixed-in positive reviews that were not actually describing a failure or regression.
